extracted_information: Automerge is a library of data structures designed for building collaborative, local-first applications. It implements a Conflict-Free Replicated Data Type (CRDT) that enables automatic merging of concurrent changes across different devices without needing a central server. It is highly flexible in its network usage, supporting various protocols including client-server, peer-to-peer, local, and even unidirectional messaging. Automerge is implemented in JavaScript and Rust, offering broad portability across platforms via FFI bindings.

features.name: Automatic Merging
features.description: As a Conflict-Free Replicated Data Type (CRDT), Automerge automatically merges concurrent changes made on different devices, eliminating the need for manual conflict resolution.

features.name: Network-agnostic
features.description: Compatible with any connection-oriented network protocol (client-server, peer-to-peer, local) and supports unidirectional messaging, such as sending files via email or file servers.

features.name: Portable
features.description: Implemented in multiple languages (JavaScript, Rust) with FFI bindings for wide compatibility across various operating systems and browser environments.

features.name: Local-first architecture
features.description: Designed for applications that prioritize local data access and operation, enabling collaboration without constant server connectivity.

core_concepts.application_type: Data structures for building collaborative applications

messaging_options: Unidirectional messaging (e.g., email attachment, file server storage)
